g.text: MENU
BACK
What is a tesseract?
What style of music is this? What's the right attitude to play it?
Why did you make nested notation? What inspired its creation,
and who inﬂuenced its development?
Is there an end to the piece?
What's the best gear to bring to a nested notation session?
A tesseract is a four-dimensional analog of the cube, just as a cube is a three-dimensional
analog of the square.
There are some mindbending animations on Wikipedia of a tesseract performing rotations,
and lots of diﬀerent projections to visualize a tesseract in diﬀerent ways.
This nested notation piece
Tesseract
is related in structure more metaphorically than literally.
The relation is true insofar as the piece forms a cube which is experienced in time, and which
connects vertices bidirectionally, therefore one dimension deeper than a cube
per se.
The relation is untrue insofar as regards the vertices: a tesseract is one dimension deeper
than the form of the nested notation piece, having twice as many vertices.
Is there any kind of admin managing each session?
